_doc: Offline / fake config for the arch-drift A/B variant. Same fake-provider trick as arch-drift.local.json but scripts TWO distinct mermaid responses keyed by model name — one each for sonnet (candidate A) and haiku (candidate B), so the report shows visibly-different markup per candidate even on fake. Both render to the SAME canned SVG (mermaid renderer is the canned shortcut to skip the mmdc dep); the demo's cost-comparison story is the load-bearing part, not the visual variation. For real per-model SVG output, use the .real.json config with mmdc installed.
_note_renderer: Set MERMAID_RENDERER=:canned in the env before running, or wrap the command (the render_mermaid transform reads it).
_note_tracer: trace.capture must include `cost` — without it the UsageAttacher's requires_capture=('cost',) check rejects at build time. The capture-check is the engine surface that catches `attach used without the data` silently-wrong, per ADR-0003.

claude-sonnet-4-6:
{"mermaid":"flowchart TD
  subgraph core
    envelope
    kind
  end
  subgraph harness
    graph_runner
    baton
  end
  subgraph adapters
    transports
    backends
    stores
  end
  harness --> core
  adapters --> core","notes":"3-layer with subgraphs: core defines the wire shape, harness owns orchestration, adapters bind to outside systems."}
claude-haiku-4-5: {"mermaid":"flowchart LR\n  core --> harness\n  harness --> adapters","notes":"Simple 3-node left-to-right. Less detail than sonnet's grouping; if you want a one-glance picture this is it, if you want layer membership use A."}
